.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:438:{"acceptance":"1. types.go:RouteRequest has a Provider field with documented semantics.\n2. NormalizeRouteRequest captures flags.Provider into req.Provider in all cases (including when it doesn't name a harness).\n3. scoreCandidate applies a soft-preference bonus when candidate's discovered provider matches req.Provider. Regression test asserts the ranking flip.\n4. 'ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx' emits a routing event on the bead recording {resolved_provider: 'vidar-omlx', route_reason: 'provider-affinity', ...} — proving provider preference flows end-to-end.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/ passes.\n\n6. **--harness × --provider interaction** (per codex + opus fresh-eyes review). NormalizeRouteRequest MUST spec the precedence explicitly:\n   - --harness alone: pin harness. Provider ignored.\n   - --provider alone: pin provider; routing picks best harness whose candidate plan includes that provider.\n   - --harness X --provider Y where X's surface is compatible with Y: pin BOTH. (Compatible = Y is a discovered provider whose /v1/models or equivalent serves a model usable by X.)\n   - --harness X --provider Y where Y is NOT compatible with X (e.g., --harness codex --provider vidar-omlx — codex is a cloud subscription harness, vidar-omlx is a local openai-compat endpoint): fail fast at normalization with a clear diagnostic ('harness X does not dispatch to provider Y; --harness and --provider are incompatible for this combination'). Do NOT silently drop one flag.\n   - Regression test covers all four cases plus the incompatible-combo fail-fast path.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"5328120be55ab6af07a5cd35c7dc7f7a22f30f96","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:05:02.251768487Z","description":"## Problem\n\nRouteRequest struct at cli/internal/agent/types.go:330-337 has no Provider field:\n\n    type RouteRequest struct {\n        Profile         string // cheap, standard, smart\n        ModelRef        string // logical catalog ref or alias\n        ModelPin        string // exact concrete model string\n        Effort          string\n        Permissions     string\n        HarnessOverride string\n    }\n\nNormalizeRouteRequest (cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305-312) only translates --provider when it names a known harness (or alias like 'local'); otherwise it DROPS the value entirely:\n\n    } else if flags.Provider != '' {\n        resolved := resolveHarnessAlias(flags.Provider)\n        // Only treat as harness override if it names a registered harness;\n        // otherwise it's a ddx-agent provider name (e.g. 'vidar') and\n        // should not constrain harness selection.\n        if _, ok := builtinHarnesses[resolved]; ok {\n            req.HarnessOverride = resolved\n        }\n        // else: provider silently discarded — RouteRequest carries no provider preference\n    }\n\nResult: the rest of the routing engine (candidate planning, scoring, escalation) has no way to see --provider. The --provider flag 'works' today only because RunOptions.Provider is passed directly to resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider at agent_runner.go:93 via a parallel code path that bypasses routing.\n\nThis is the root cause of every gap SD-015 calls 'provider soft preference' — Mode 2 step 3 ('pick best provider'), Mode 3 ('provider soft preference'), Mode 5 ('--provider P alone'). None can work without a Provider field in RouteRequest.\n\n## Fix\n\n1. Add Provider string field to RouteRequest (types.go:330). Document its semantics: 'preferred provider name (soft affinity for candidate ranking; hard affinity when combined with --harness or when it's the only input)'.\n\n2. Update NormalizeRouteRequest (routing.go:305-312) to ALWAYS capture flags.Provider into req.Provider regardless of harness naming. Keep the existing harness-alias resolution separately.\n\n3. Teach scoreCandidate (routing.go:471-550) to award a bonus when the candidate's concrete provider matches req.Provider (SD-015 'Provider as Modifier' soft preference).\n\n4. Teach BuildCandidatePlans (routing.go:27+) to preserve the discovered provider on each CandidatePlan so scoreCandidate has the data to do the comparison.\n\n5. Regression test: RouteRequest{Provider: 'vidar-omlx', ModelPin: 'Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit'} produces a candidate plan that ranks vidar-omlx (embedded-openai surface) above other providers with the same model, and CandidatePlan carries Provider='vidar-omlx'.\n\n## Files likely touched\n\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — RouteRequest + CandidatePlan.Provider (CandidatePlan already has Provider per earlier work)\n- cli/internal/agent/routing.go — NormalizeRouteRequest, scoreCandidate, evaluateCandidate (for assigning Provider to plans)\n- cli/internal/agent/routing_test.go — Provider propagation + soft-preference scoring tests\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — ensure --provider reaches both RouteFlags.Provider and RunOptions.Provider as today\n\n## Depends on\n\nNone. This is the foundational fix; everything else in the epic depends on it.","events_attachment":"ddx-8610020e/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent: provider error: POST \"http://bragi:1234/v1/chat/completions\": 502 Bad Gateway ","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-19T03:26:21Z","id":"ddx-8610020e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:bug","area:agent","area:routing"],"notes":"Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing","parent":"ddx-2d974641","priority":1,"spec-id":"SD-015","status":"closed","title":"routing: add Provider field to RouteRequest; --provider is silently dropped at normalization","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:46.694249942Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:447:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator using a browser — on a machine without a terminal, or a phone, or a laptop away from my dev box — I can open my project's home page, type a prompt into a chat pane, and see the DDx agent work on it in real time. Tool calls stream as they happen, costs accrue visibly, and the conversation persists so I can come back to it.\n\n**Acceptance criteria (written for Option A; rewrite if design note chooses B/C/D):**\n\n1. **Design note landed.** One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` records the chosen option, the answers to the six subsidiary questions, and the message/thread schema. Implementation PR links to it.\n\n2. **Thread model.** Chat threads persist under `.ddx/chat/\u003cthread-id\u003e/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}`. One thread per project per operator by default; operators can create named threads. Messages.jsonl is append-only, one JSON object per message (user or assistant), with `sessionId` linking each assistant turn back to an entry in the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`).\n\n3. **GraphQL surface.** New resolvers:\n   - Query: `chatThreads(projectId)`, `chatThread(id)`, `chatMessages(threadId, since?)`.\n   - Mutation: `chatThreadCreate(projectId, title?)`, `chatSend(threadId, message, harness?, profile?)` → kicks off a session via `service.Execute`.\n   - Subscription: `chatStream(threadId)` → emits assistant message chunks + tool call events (same event vocabulary as `workers.recentEvents`).\n\n4. **Home page UI.** Project home page gains a chat pane below the actions section. Pane shows thread list, active thread messages, input box, harness/profile picker. Tool calls render inline within assistant messages. Streaming updates as chunks arrive.\n\n5. **Session linkage.** Each assistant turn creates one session in the sessions index. Clicking an assistant message shows the session id and a link to the session detail (`/sessions/\u003cid\u003e`). Chat thread header shows cumulative session count + total cost for the thread.\n\n6. **Concurrency.** Two browsers open on the same thread see each other's messages within 2s (subscription-driven). Two operators on different threads in the same project do not interfere.\n\n7. **Interrupt.** The in-flight assistant turn can be cancelled via a Stop button; server sends the harness the equivalent of `ddx agent workers stop \u003cid\u003e` for the in-flight session. The thread records a cancelled turn, does not try to auto-resume.\n\n8. **Cost guardrail (soft).** If the cumulative thread cost passes a configurable ceiling (`.ddx/config.yaml: chat.cost_ceiling_usd`, default unset), the UI warns but does not block. Hard block is a follow-up.\n\n9. **Auth posture.**\n   - Per design note. If the chosen posture is \"single-operator localhost / tsnet only for v1,\" the chat routes are served unauthenticated behind tsnet and the design note records the gap to close before broader deployment.\n   - A small middleware gate is in place even for v1 so adding auth later is schema-local, not route-local.\n\n10. **Playwright e2e.**\n    - Seeds a project with zero chat threads.\n    - Navigates to project home, asserts the chat pane renders with \"Start a new thread\" affordance.\n    - Starts a thread, sends a short prompt, simulates a streamed session response with one tool call, asserts chunks appear in order and the session id link is present.\n    - Opens a second browser context on the same thread, asserts both see new messages within 2s.\n    - Cancels an in-flight turn, asserts the cancelled state is persisted.\n\n11. **Depends on.**\n    - `ddx-2513f08c` (home page reachable from sidebar) — hard dependency.\n    - `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) — hard dependency for the session linkage + cost rollup.\n\n12. **Explicitly deferred.** PTY integration (Option B / pop-out terminal from Option C) is its own bead, filed only if the Option A rollout surfaces real interactive-steering needs.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"9c29b5fe9cb5e06c3a0f1ca3d8133f8766b59259","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:51:38.374211227Z","description":"## Observed / Motivation\n\nUser wants to \"lift work out of the TUI entirely and allow non-tmux users to work remotely.\" Today the only way to prompt the DDx agent stack interactively is a terminal session. The web UI is read-mostly: dispatch three actions (drain/align/checks) and review results. There is no surface that lets an operator say \"go do X\" against the project in natural language from a browser.\n\nRelated UX: the project home page already exists (`/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/`), renders queue summary + actions, and is the natural host for a chat affordance. First the home page needs to be reachable from the sidebar (`ddx-2513f08c`) — this bead depends on that work.\n\n## The tension\n\nThe DDx harness abstraction (`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go`, `service.Execute`) is designed for **non-interactive** use: prompt in, structured result out, optional streaming. This is the right shape for queue-draining, quorum, evaluation, and automation. It is **not** the shape of a TUI conversation with tab completion, mid-run steering, and back-channel signals.\n\nAn operator-facing chat can land on either side of that line. The bead records all four options so the design phase can pick one deliberately.\n\n## Options (design phase decides, recommended path noted)\n\n### Option A — Chat over the harness abstraction (RECOMMENDED starting point)\n\n- Chat UI posts a prompt → server dispatches a session via `service.Execute` (or a wrapper) → streams tool calls + response chunks back over a GraphQL subscription.\n- Message history is persisted as a thread; each user turn = one new session. Responses reuse the existing `recentEvents` stream shape already used on the workers detail page.\n- Works with every harness today because the harness abstraction is what it is. claude, codex, agent (embedded) all participate.\n- **Trade-off:** no mid-run steering, no tab completion, no live back-and-forth inside one agent invocation.\n- Matches the user's stated target audience (non-TUI users who want remote browser access). Those users aren't trying to steer a TUI — they're trying to leave it behind.\n\n### Option B — PTY gateway per harness\n\n- Server owns a PTY pool. Each chat thread attaches to one harness process (claude/codex/etc.) over a WebSocket; browser renders xterm.js.\n- Full interactivity, including TUI steering.\n- **Trade-off:** PTY lifecycle, auth, resource limits, tab-close-loses-session semantics, multi-user access control — all real. The harness abstraction is bypassed for this mode, which creates a second execution path to maintain.\n\n### Option C — Hybrid (chat-first + pop-out terminal)\n\n- Option A is the primary surface. When a chat reaches a point that needs interactive steering, a \"Open terminal\" escape hatch attaches a PTY to the current harness session.\n- **Trade-off:** more surface area, but honest about both use cases. Most sessions stay on the cheap path.\n\n### Option D — Web-tmux / ttyd wrapper\n\n- Embed the existing terminal tooling directly: ttyd or xterm.js over a tmux socket owned by the server.\n- **Trade-off:** fastest to ship, zero harness work, but the UX is a terminal in a browser — not \"chat.\" Requires tmux. Doesn't serve the stated \"non-tmux users\" audience.\n\n### Recommendation\n\nStart with **A**. If the design note documents real interactive-steering needs we can't paper over, the right follow-up is **C** (A + PTY escape hatch), not B (PTY-only).\n\n## Scope of this bead\n\n- Design note in `docs/helix/02-design/` locking the option, the message schema, persistence model, and auth posture. Implementation PR links to it.\n- First implementation pass: Option A.\n- Home page gains a chat pane as its primary interaction surface, alongside the existing queue summary + actions (which stay — they're quick-dispatch affordances, not replaced by chat).\n\n## Subsidiary questions the design note must answer\n\n1. **Message persistence.** Are chat threads per-project or per-user? Stored where (`.ddx/chat/\u003cthread-id\u003e/` or elsewhere)? Is history shared across operators on the same project, or isolated per operator identity?\n2. **Harness selection.** Does the chat pick a harness per-thread (picker in the UI) or per-message (re-route each turn)? Default to profile-based selection?\n3. **Cost accountability.** Every chat message spawns a session. Sessions are already tracked in the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). The chat thread should surface cumulative cost to the operator so they don't blow budget unknowingly.\n4. **Tool execution scope.** Chat prompts will trigger agent tool calls that read/write the project tree. Is the chat session bounded to the same permissions as `ddx agent run`? Does it run in the project worktree, a worktree per thread, a scratch dir? Default safe answer: same worktree as `ddx agent run` (user trusts ddx running anyway), but design note records it.\n5. **Concurrency.** What happens if two operators have the chat open on the same project and both send messages? Default: independent threads, each with its own session — no shared state.\n6. **Auth.** Today the server assumes single-operator localhost. Remote use implies some identity concept. Design note picks a posture: delegate to reverse proxy, ship a simple token, or punt and require tsnet + single-operator for v1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Full PTY implementation (Option B). Follow-up bead if A+hybrid doesn't suffice.\n- Cross-project chat.\n- Voice input.","events_attachment":"ddx-88e91fdb/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-88e91fdb","issue_type":"epic","labels":["feat-008","feat-006","chat","remote-ops","design"],"priority":2,"session_id":"eb-45976f78","status":"closed","title":"Add chat interface to project home page: prompt ddx remotely without the TUI","updated_at":"2026-04-28T22:40:26.03095778Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:607:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator on any page in the project, I always see whether a drain worker is running and how much work is queued. One click takes me to the worker overview where I can scale capacity up or down with a single button, without picking a harness or profile every time.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Nav indicator visible everywhere.**\n   - A component in `NavShell.svelte` renders the drain indicator on every route inside a selected project.\n   - Idle state: dot + \"Queue: X ready\".\n   - Active state: spinner + \"N workers · X ready\".\n   - Clicking navigates to the workers overview.\n   - Playwright: navigate to beads, documents, sessions, efficacy — assert indicator is present on each with the expected text.\n\n2. **Live updates.**\n   - The indicator updates within 2s when a worker starts, finishes, or changes state. Backed by the existing worker-progress subscription.\n   - When ready-bead count changes (a bead is created/closed), the indicator updates within 5s. Either subscription-driven or short poll.\n\n3. **Summary query.**\n   - New `queueAndWorkersSummary(projectId) { readyBeads runningWorkers totalWorkers }`. Returns zeros on unknown or empty projects — no error.\n   - Resolver is cheap: reads the ready count from the same path bead-status queries use, not by iterating every bead.\n\n4. **Overview page — count control.**\n   - Header shows \"Drain workers: N\" prominently.\n   - `+ Add worker` button dispatches a default-spec worker (`workerDispatch(kind: \"execute-loop\", args: null)`). On success the row appears in the table within 2s and the indicator updates.\n   - `− Remove worker` button stops the oldest-running drain worker. Confirm-gated (\"Stop worker-XYZ?\"). After confirm, the worker transitions to `stopped` within 2s.\n   - Help text below the buttons: \"Adds a general-purpose drain worker. Use the per-harness picker below for custom specs.\"\n\n5. **Default-spec + safety rail.**\n   - `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.default_spec` is respected by `+ Add worker`. Integration test asserts a configured `profile: cheap` is propagated.\n   - `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.max_count`: when set, `+ Add worker` refuses past the cap; button disabled with tooltip \"at workers.max_count limit\". Not set → no cap. Integration test covers both.\n\n6. **Playwright — flow.**\n   - Seeds a project with 3 ready beads, zero workers.\n   - Asserts indicator shows \"Queue: 3 ready\", dot state.\n   - Clicks indicator → navigates to overview.\n   - Clicks `+ Add worker` → asserts new row and indicator transitions to spinner + \"1 worker · 3 ready\".\n   - Clicks `+` again → asserts \"2 workers · 3 ready\".\n   - Clicks `− Remove worker` → asserts one worker stops and indicator shows \"1 worker · ...\" within 2s.\n   - Navigates to /beads → asserts indicator persists and stays live.\n\n7. **No regressions.**\n   - Existing Drain button on project home continues to work (dispatches the same kind). Can stay or be removed depending on design note; recommend keeping it as the initial-kickoff affordance when zero workers run.\n   - Existing Workers table rendering + live-phase subscription is unchanged.\n   - Existing per-worker Start/Stop from `ddx-69789664` coexists unchanged.\n\n8. **Cross-references.**\n   - Hard dependency: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (fixes `workersByProject` filter — without it, the running-workers count is always 0 on a per-project query).\n   - Adjacent: `ddx-69789664` (per-worker lifecycle + Sessions IA). These beads are complementary; implementation should not re-introduce either's functionality, just use it.\n   - Adjacent: `ddx-9ce6842a` (perf harness). The `queueAndWorkersSummary` resolver gets a perf target in that harness: p95 ≤ 30ms in-process on a 5k-bead fixture. No full scan allowed.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-25T09:52:17Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"dbc9c373185249904bec9cd32e0409c7cb9743c0","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:03:03.880032436Z","description":"## Observed / Motivation\n\nToday the queue-drain worker is invisible unless the operator is on the project home page or the Workers tab. An operator on any other page (Beads, Documents, Efficacy) has no idea whether a drain is active, how many ready beads are left, or whether they should kick off more capacity. The per-project home page's \"Drain queue\" button is one-shot and offers no scaling control — you start one worker and that's it.\n\nUser feedback: the drain should be a **top-level always-visible affordance** with:\n1. A persistent spinner/badge in the global nav showing drain state + an at-a-glance count (ready beads, running workers).\n2. Clicking the badge goes to a workers overview where the operator can see what each worker is doing.\n3. That overview needs a \"number of workers\" control — `Add worker` / `Remove worker` to scale drain capacity up or down, not just start-one-at-a-time.\n\n## Why this is a new surface, not just a tweak to existing Workers page\n\n- **Global spinner** is a nav-shell concern, not a page concern. It needs to be mounted in `NavShell.svelte` so every route sees it.\n- **Worker-count control** is a different interaction model from the per-worker Start/Stop flow proposed in `ddx-69789664`. That flow is \"pick a harness + effort + filter, start one worker with those specs.\" This flow is \"I want N general-purpose drain workers; add/remove one at a time.\" Both should coexist.\n- A **worker-count affordance** implies a default worker spec (default harness, default profile). That's fine — it mirrors `ddx work` with no flags — but it has to be explicit so the operator knows what they're spawning.\n\n## Scope\n\n### Part 1 — Global drain indicator in `NavShell`\n\n- New element in the top nav bar, visible on every route (fits next to the theme toggle).\n- Two states:\n  - **Idle** (zero running drain workers): subtle dot + \"Queue: X ready\" (where X is project-scoped ready-bead count). Clicking navigates to the workers view.\n  - **Active** (≥1 running drain worker): animated spinner + \"N workers · X ready\". Clicking navigates to the workers view.\n- Data: new lightweight query `queueAndWorkersSummary(projectId) { readyBeads, runningWorkers, totalWorkers }`, subscribes to worker-progress events to stay fresh.\n- Scope: tied to the currently-selected project in the `ProjectPicker`. If no project is selected, the indicator is hidden.\n- Perf: the query must be cheap — must not trigger the N·M scan already tracked in `ddx-9ce6842a`. Depends on those fixes for the ready-bead count.\n\n### Part 2 — Workers overview with count control\n\n- Repurpose the existing `/workers` route (or add a separate `/workers/overview` — design call) to include a header section with:\n  - Large \"Drain workers: N\" number.\n  - `+` button (\"Add worker\") — dispatches a new default-spec drain worker via the existing `workerDispatch(kind: \"execute-loop\")` mutation.\n  - `−` button (\"Remove worker\") — stops the oldest-running drain worker. Confirm-gated.\n  - Small help text: \"Adds a general-purpose queue-drain worker. Use the per-harness picker below for custom specs.\"\n- The existing per-worker table and per-row Start/Stop (from `ddx-69789664`) remain for fine control.\n- \"Default spec\" is: harness unset (route via profile), profile `default`, no effort override. Operator can change the project default in config.\n\n### Part 3 — Defaults + config\n\n- `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.default_spec` — object with optional harness, profile, effort, min_tier, max_tier. Used by Part 2's `+ Add worker`. Unset = use `ddx work` defaults.\n- `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.max_count` — optional safety rail, default unset. When set, `+ Add worker` refuses past the cap and tooltips \"at workers.max_count limit\".\n\n### Part 4 — Empty/error states\n\n- Indicator on a project with no ready beads and zero workers: \"Queue: 0 ready — nothing to drain\". Clicking still navigates (so operator can spawn one anyway).\n- Indicator when the per-project workers query is broken (pre-`ddx-05b4cc9d`): falls back to the global count; logs a one-time console warning.\n- Add-worker failure: surfaces inline on the overview page (error toast + detail), doesn't silently drop.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Per-harness-specific queue views (e.g., \"drain with codex\" as a distinct indicator).\n- Bead prioritization rules for multi-worker draining — beads already have a priority field, and `execute-loop` already claims in ready-order. No change.\n- Concurrency safety review of multi-worker draining. It already works (beads are claimed atomically), but if `ddx-05b4cc9d` surfaces issues there, file a follow-up.","events_attachment":"ddx-b6cf025c/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-25T09:59:17.30531356Z","feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-b6cf025c","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-006","ui","operator-ux"],"notes":"**`+layout.svelte` (workers overview)**: The `+ Add worker` button has no awareness of `workers.max_count`. This needs the `queueAndWorkersSummary` query (or a new field) to expose the cap, and the button's `disabled` binding to incorporate it.\n**`workers.spec.ts`**: The e2e test asserts `dispatchCalled` and `stopCalled` booleans but does not verify the **indicator text transitions** after add/remove. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:628:{"acceptance":"1. .ddx/config.yaml schema supports profile_ladders (per-profile ordered tier list) AND the legacy profile_priority is still readable with a deprecation warning.\n\n2. Four shipped profile ladders: default=[cheap,standard,smart], cheap=[cheap], fast=[fast,smart], smart=[smart]. Documented in FEAT-006.\n\n3. Tier escalation in routing.go / execute_bead_orchestrator.go iterates profile_ladders[P] on failure, respecting --provider affinity (ddx-3c5ba7cc) and capability gating (ddx-4817edfd). Escalation triggers: execution_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed, structural_validation_failed. no_changes uses the existing cooldown path.\n\n4. Bead routing event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. A run that escalates cheap -\u003e standard -\u003e success at standard has an event with escalation_count=1, resolved_tier=standard, final_tier=standard.\n\n5. 'ddx work --profile default' drains the queue by trying local first and escalating only when needed. Measured outcome over a test run of 10 representative beads shows at least 50% closing at the cheap tier (target: most work lands locally; escalations are the exception).\n\n6. Regression tests cover each ladder path: success-at-cheap, escalate-cheap-to-standard, escalate-all-tiers-then-exhaustion, --max-tier caps the ladder, --profile cheap never escalates.\n\n7. FEAT-006 Profile Routing section updated with the ladder semantics and the intent table (default/cheap/fast/smart).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"93f938c41c93be78fc199bd150f455add6575d24","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:51:49.512908486Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-2d974641","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:52:01Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-e7254128","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:58:09Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-7bc0c8d5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:36:21Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-ea7f041c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:36:16Z"}],"description":"## Target UX\n\nThe user-facing profile vocabulary (cheap / default / fast / smart) should map to concrete routing policies that take full advantage of local models without blocking on their limits. Specifically:\n\n### default profile (the common case)\nTry local models first, escalate to cloud if:\n- local model's context window is too small for the bead prompt\n- local model lacks a capability the bead requires (tools, structured output, etc.)\n- local dispatch fails (provider unreachable, timeout, model-side error)\n- bead reopens due to post-run check failure (review-malfunction, land-conflict, etc.)\n\nEscalation target: next tier up in the ladder (standard cloud — e.g. codex gpt-5.4 within subscription). If THAT fails, escalate once more to smart cloud. Then stop and unclaim.\n\n### cheap profile\nLocal only. Never escalate. Intended for high-volume exploratory work where cost dominates and adequate-quality is sufficient. If the cheap tier can't serve the bead, the bead unclaims and waits.\n\n### fast profile\nCloud-first, skip local entirely. Intended for latency-critical or interactive work where local inference's 9s warmup is unacceptable. Starts at a fast cloud model (e.g. gemini-flash or haiku) and escalates to smart on failure.\n\n### smart profile\nCloud-high-quality from the start. Intended for bead-review, adversarial checks, or beads whose AC demands high-quality reasoning. No escalation — if smart fails, the work fails meaningfully.\n\n## Why this matters (dogfooding signal)\n\nAs of 2026-04-18, DDx's default is cheap = qwen/qwen3.6 local (vidar-omlx). With v0.3.14 shipping the SSE-comment-frame filter, the local path actually works end-to-end. The queue can drain mostly on local hardware for pennies per bead, escalating only when the bead genuinely needs cloud quality. That's the throughput-per-dollar goal (goal:throughput-per-dollar label).\n\n## Prereqs (already filed under ddx-2d974641 or sibling beads)\n\nThis epic is blocked on the autorouting epic delivering its exit-gate UX — 'declare provider + use canonical ref + autorouting works'. Specifically:\n\n- ddx-8610020e (RouteRequest.Provider field) — foundation\n- ddx-0486e601 (fuzzy normalization + per-provider) — lets cheap-tier model refs resolve to provider-native IDs\n- ddx-2f5a2284 (catalog/Profile discovery integration) — lets Profile tier overrides retarget to provider-native IDs\n- ddx-3c5ba7cc (tier escalation --provider affinity) — the lower half of this epic's 'escalate on failure' semantics\n- ddx-4817edfd (capability gating) — prevents routing to a model that can't serve the bead\n\nWith those in place, the profile-level policy here is mostly CONFIGURATION + a small amount of scoring-layer logic.\n\n## Scope\n\n### Config schema additions\n\n.ddx/config.yaml agent.routing extends with an explicit per-profile ladder:\n\n    agent:\n      routing:\n        profile_ladders:\n          default: [cheap, standard, smart]      # local first, escalate\n          cheap:   [cheap]                        # local only\n          fast:    [fast, smart]                  # cloud fast, escalate to smart\n          smart:   [smart]                        # cloud high-quality, no escalation\n        model_overrides:\n          cheap:    qwen/qwen3.6\n          fast:     kimi/k2.5\n          standard: codex/gpt-5.4                # new: subscription cloud, mid-quality\n          smart:    minimax/minimax-m2.7\n\nCurrent profile_priority field (a flat list) becomes a legacy alias for profile_ladders.default with a deprecation warning.\n\n### Tier-escalation policy (routing.go)\n\nFor a given profile P, iterate profile_ladders[P]:\n- Evaluate model_overrides[tier] resolves on a viable candidate (with --provider affinity from ddx-3c5ba7cc).\n- If yes, try it; on failure (execution_failed / land_conflict / post_run_check_failed), move to next tier.\n- Record tier decision + escalation chain in routing event on the bead so telemetry reflects where work actually landed.\n\n### User-facing CLI\n\n- ddx work --profile default — the new primary invocation. Most users run this.\n- ddx work --profile cheap / fast / smart — opt-in deviations.\n- --max-tier continues to cap the ladder (explicit user override beats config).\n\n### Telemetry + measurement\n\nEvery execution event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. Aggregated metrics (FEAT-016 / ddx-4b59b816) report per-profile throughput, per-tier cost, per-bead escalation rate.\n\n## Exit gate\n\n'ddx work --profile default --once --local' against a ready bead on a host with a healthy local provider AND healthy cloud fallback:\n- Claims the bead.\n- Tries cheap tier (local). If it succeeds, closes bead with tier=cheap recorded.\n- If cheap fails for any escalation-triggering reason, automatically retries at standard tier without operator intervention.\n- If standard fails, retries at smart.\n- Final outcome recorded faithfully (success at whatever tier landed, or exhaustion if all tiers failed).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Dynamic profile selection ('auto-pick profile based on bead complexity'). Too much magic; explicit profile choice is fine.\n- Cost budgets / circuit breakers. FEAT-014 (token awareness) tracks this separately.\n- Per-bead profile overrides (bead-level 'requires-profile: smart'). Could be a future follow-up but out of scope here.\n\n## Metadata\n\nGoverning spec: FEAT-006 (agent service) + the autorouting epic ddx-2d974641. This bead is the PROFILE-layer policy that sits on top of the routing fixes from ddx-2d974641. Do not start until ddx-2d974641's Phase-1 children are closed.","events_attachment":"ddx-bbb65768/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-bbb65768","issue_type":"epic","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:design","area:agent","area:routing","area:profiles","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:deferred","workstream:agent-upgrade"],"priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"profile/automation: default = local-first with escalation; fast/smart = cloud-first","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:03:22.145529072Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:843:{"acceptance":"**User story:** After the upstream point release lands and DDx pulls it, `ddx agent run` with no flags just works on my machine. `--profile local`, `--harness claude`, `--model opus-4.7` behave per the upstream contract. The drain-queue failure mode (empty spec → gemini+minimax → 19-burn) is impossible.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Upstream prerequisite tagged and pulled.** Go.mod references a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class `default` and `local` profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs + gating; `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible`; public decision-trace (`RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error); cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` with unknown-cost fallback. All upstream beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` labeled `routing-plan` are closed.\n\n2. **No DDx-side synthesis on default path.**\n   - `ddx agent run` with zero flags issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call, no Model, no Harness, no Provider.\n   - `ddx work` default: same shape.\n   - `ddx agent execute-bead` with no overrides: same shape.\n   - Integration test: same config that caused the drain-queue failure (real `~/.local/share/ddx/.ddx/config.yaml`) now either succeeds or returns a single typed error — never 19-burn.\n   - Unit test: `workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker({})` → spec with `Profile: \"default\"`, no other fields. No `ResolveTierModelRef`, no `ResolveProfileLadder` in the call graph on the default path.\n\n3. **Preflight before claim.**\n   - Call sequence at `execute_bead_loop.go:352`: next-candidate → `ResolveRoute` → (preflight gate, new) → `Claim` → invoke. Route error OR preflight rejection exits the loop with a `worker-level` failure — no bead claimed.\n   - Test: seed a pathological route (harness with allow-list that excludes the decision's model — fabricated via upstream test seam). Assert `beadStore.Claim` is never called; worker record shows `status: execution_failed` with detail naming the rejected pair; no bead has a `kind:tier-attempt` event from this attempt.\n\n4. **Config deprecation is deliberate, not warning-only.**\n   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.default_harness` set produces a **hard error** at startup with a migration pointer. Field is deleted from the config schema.\n   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.profile_ladders` or `agent.routing.model_overrides` set produces a one-time warning per process AND causes the default path to ignore them. Opt-in with `--escalate` or `--override-model` explicit flags.\n   - CHANGELOG entry + migration guide in DDx docs.\n   - Migration test: fixture config containing all three deprecated fields, run `ddx agent run` with no flags, assert warnings printed and default path honored (not the deprecated config).\n\n5. **Override behavior matches G3 exactly.**\n   - `--harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Harness: \"claude\")`. If claude fails upstream's gates (binary missing, quota exhausted), surfaces the typed error; no silent substitution. E2E test mocks each gate failure.\n   - `--model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Model: \"opus-4.7\")`. Lowest-cost available quota-OK provider wins. E2E test seeds 3 providers at different cost with shared model; asserts lowest-cost pick.\n   - `--harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` when combo is invalid; clean dispatch otherwise.\n   - `--profile local --harness claude` → upstream typed error (\"claude is non-local, profile=local is local-only\"); DDx surfaces it unmodified. E2E test.\n\n6. **Playwright — drain-queue goldens.**\n   - Fixture matches user's real config (endpoints: lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio, no gemini binary test or with gemini installed but not in allow-list for the chosen model).\n   - Click Drain on project home → within 5s: worker row appears with harness = `agent` (or another live-endpoint-backed harness). Never gemini + non-gemini model. Never 19 failures.\n   - After drain completes: worker status `success` (or typed error exit with reason recorded on the bead). Attempts count matches actual work done.\n\n7. **No compensating DDx logic retained.**\n   - Search DDx for code that re-implements any of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review.\n   - `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`, `workersByHarness` escalation helpers are deleted from the default path. They remain reachable via `--escalate` only. Dead-code detector run in CI catches orphans.\n\n8. **D6 / D7 are explicitly out of scope and filed as follow-ups.**\n   - `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter) remains its own bead and may land before or after this one.\n   - A new bead is filed post-release for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer work, pointing at A4 upstream as satisfied.\n\n9. **Documentation.**\n   - DDx's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md links to upstream profile catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract docs. No re-documentation on the DDx side.\n   - DDx CHANGELOG entry covers the go.mod bump, D4 breaking changes, and new CLI surface semantics.\n\n10. **Regression goldens for the drain-queue bug.**\n    - Integration test exists that reproduces the original failure mode (empty spec + user's config + gemini binary installed + minimax model in overrides). Before this bead's work: test fails with 19-burn. After: test succeeds or exits with one clean error. Test stays in the suite as a regression guard.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:26:11.505315747Z","description":"## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)\n\nOriginal plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.\n\n## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)\n\n**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.\n\n**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.\n\n**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.\n\n## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)\n\nThis bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:\n\n- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.\n- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.\n- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.\n- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for \"no viable candidate\" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.\n- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.\n- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and \"best provider\" contract.\n\nOnce that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.\n\n## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)\n\n**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**\n\n`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: \"default\"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond \"profile=default if unset.\"\n\n**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**\n\nDefault `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.\n\n**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**\n\nAfter `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.\n\n**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**\n\nDeliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:\n- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.\n- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.\n- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.\n- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.\n\nMigration note published in DDx changelog.\n\n**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**\n\n- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")`. No other DDx inputs.\n- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.\n- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Harness: \"claude\")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.\n- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Model: \"opus-4.7\")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.\n- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.\n- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.\n\n## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)\n\n- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).\n- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.\n\n## Sequencing\n\n1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)\n2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.\n3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.\n4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.\n5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.\n\n## Cross-references\n\n- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.\n- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.\n- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.\n- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): \"kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match.\"\n- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):\n  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)\n  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)\n  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)\n- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).\n\n## Codex review — key corrections folded in\n\n1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just \"fix ExactPin semantics.\"\n2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.\n3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.\n4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.\n5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.\n6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).\n7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.\n8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.\n9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.\n10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.","events_attachment":"ddx-fdd3ea36/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: stalled: no_progress_tools_exceeded","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":4,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-30T00:06:21Z","feature":"FEAT-006","id":"ddx-fdd3ea36","issue_type":"epic","labels":["feat-006","feat-010","routing","upstream-agent","design"],"notes":"SUPERSEDED by ddx-c4231775 after fresh-eyes review findings.\n\nThis epic encoded the older plan where DDx called ResolveRoute in execution paths, accepted default/local profiles, preserved harness/model override semantics, and kept preflight/candidate-trace behavior in the work path. .ddx/attachments/ddx-88e91fdb/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead: ddx-88e91fdb — Add chat interface to project home page\n\nDisposition: no commit this pass; recommend decomposing into 4–5 smaller beads.\n\n(a) What is done in this pass\n- Read the bead description and 12 acceptance criteria.\n- Mapped the existing surface area:\n  - GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql with gqlgen-generated resolvers; subscription pattern established in resolver_sub_worker.go / resolver_sub_bead.go / resolver_sub_exec.go (buffered channel + ctx-cancel goroutine).\n  - Frontend GraphQL via graphql-request (queries) + graphql-ws (subscriptions) — see cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/gql/{client.ts,subscriptions.ts}. No Houdini.\n  - Project home page at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte (queue summary + 3 action cards). This is the host surface for the chat pane.\n  - Sessions index at cli/internal/agent/session_index.go, sharded JSONL under .ddx/sessions/ (monthly). agentSessions GraphQL query already exists. This is the linkage target for AC5.\n  - Agent execution entry at cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunWithConfigViaService → executeOnService → records a session-index row). Streaming today is per-harness; the execute-loop streams events via worker progress, not per-prompt chunks.\n  - Playwright e2e pattern in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/**/*.e2e.ts uses page.route('/graphql') interception with fixture data; no live backend.\n\n(b) What is blocking a one-pass implementation\nThe bead bundles a feature that fans out across at least 5 independently-reviewable surfaces:\n\n  1. Storage layer (.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}) — new Go package with tests, append-only invariants, project/operator scoping.\n  2. GraphQL schema + resolvers — chatThreads/chatThread/chatMessages queries, chatThreadCreate/chatSend mutations, chatStream subscription. Requires schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regeneration of generated.go, models.go updates, four new resolver files, integration tests.\n  3. service.Execute integration — chatSend must dispatch a session and stream chunks + tool calls back over the chatStream subscription. Today service.Execute is request/response with a non-streaming Result; per-chunk streaming over the subscription bus is new infrastructure. Either: (i) add a streaming variant of executeOnService, or (ii) tail the session's evidence stream and re-emit. Both have non-trivial design choices that belong in the design note before code lands.\n  4. Frontend — chat pane component, thread list, harness/profile picker, streaming render, tool-call inline rendering, Stop button (AC7), cumulative cost header (AC5/AC8). Real-time updates via subscribeChatMessages helper. Roughly parallel in size to the workers detail page.\n  5. Auth posture middleware (AC9) — even as a \"single-operator localhost / tsnet for v1\" stance, the design note must commit to a posture and a route-gate stub must exist so future auth is schema-local. The bead description flags this as load-bearing for the \"remote ops\" framing.\n  6. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ answering the six subsidiary questions (persistence scope, harness selection, cost accountability, tool-execution scope, concurrency, auth). The bead's AC1 makes this a hard prerequisite — the implementation PR is supposed to *link* to a landed design note. The note must precede or accompany the implementation; it cannot be a postscript.\n  7. Playwright e2e (AC10) — five-step scenario including two browser contexts on the same thread, cancel mid-flight, streamed chunk ordering. Mocking the GraphQL subscription side of this is non-trivial; the existing e2e suite mocks queries via page.route but does not exercise graphql-ws subscriptions.\n\nA single execute-bead commit covering all of the above would either (i) be partial across most of those surfaces and red, or (ii) take many hours of focused work and produce a sprawling diff that the review gate cannot meaningfully verify in one pass. Both outcomes are worse than a clean decomposition.\n\nAdditional concrete unknowns the design note must resolve before code can land cleanly:\n- Is the chatStream subscription chunk-level (assistant text deltas) or event-level (one event per tool call + one final assistant message)? This determines whether service.Execute needs a new streaming surface or can be wrapped post-hoc.\n- For multi-operator on the same thread (AC6), is the second operator a read-only observer or an equal participant who can also send? The bead defaults to \"two browsers on the same thread see each other within 2s\" which implies multi-participant — but the cost-accountability and session-attribution model needs a per-message author identity that today's auth posture (single-operator) does not surface.\n- Does chatSend run in the project worktree or a per-thread worktree? Bead suggests \"same as ddx agent run\" as the default, but if two threads chatSend concurrently they will race the worktree. This argues for per-thread worktree, which is a real cost (disk, fs setup) and belongs in the design note.\n\n(c) What a follow-up attempt would need\n\nRecommend splitting this bead into the following sub-beads, executed in order:\n\n  Sub-bead 1: Design note (docs/helix/02-design/chat-interface.md)\n    - Picks Option A vs B vs C vs D and locks it.\n    - Answers the six subsidiary questions.\n    - Specifies the message/thread schema (manifest.json shape, messages.jsonl entry shape).\n    - Specifies whether chatStream is chunk-level or event-level.\n    - Specifies worktree scoping per thread.\n    - Specifies auth posture for v1 and the gap to broader deployment.\n    Size: 1 pass, design only. No code.\n\n  Sub-bead 2: Chat thread storage package (cli/internal/chat/)\n    - New Go package: ChatThread, ChatMessage, AppendMessage, LoadThread, ListThreads.\n    - Append-only messages.jsonl invariants, atomic manifest writes.\n    - Unit tests including concurrent append safety.\n    Size: 1 pass.\n\n  Sub-bead 3: GraphQL surface (schema + resolvers + integration tests)\n    - schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regen, four resolver files, integration tests.\n    - chatSend dispatches via service.Execute and writes the assistant turn as a single message once the session completes (event-level streaming, simplest first cut). Chunk-level streaming is a follow-up.\n    - chatStream subscription emits message-created events.\n    - Session linkage via SessionIndexEntry.id reference on each assistant message.\n    Size: 1 pass.\n\n  Sub-bead 4: Frontend chat pane\n    - New ChatPane.svelte component, thread list, message list, input box, harness/profile picker.\n    - Mounted on project home page below the actions section.\n    - subscribeChatMessages helper in src/lib/gql/subscriptions.ts.\n    - Cost rollup in thread header. Stop button calls workerStop on the in-flight session's worker.\n    Size: 1 pass.\n\n  Sub-bead 5: Playwright e2e + auth posture middleware\n    - Two-context test, cancel test, streaming chunk test (with mocked graphql-ws server).\n    - Auth middleware gate stub on chat routes.\n    Size: 1 pass.\n\nThe bead's AC11 (depends-on ddx-2513f08c, ddx-2ceb02fa) was not verified in this pass. Sub-bead 1 should re-verify those deps before locking the design.\n\nRecommendation: re-queue this bead as a tracking parent and create the five sub-beads above. Playwright test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:349 (\"plugin update falls\n  back to polling when worker stream drops terminal event\") exercises this.\n\nAC4 (in-flight visual state):\n  +page.svelte lines 377-383. Loader2 spinner + \"Installing...\"/\"Updating...\"\n  label replaces the status badge while workerId && action are set. .ddx/attachments/ddx-6860e1ea/events.jsonl:2:{"actor":"opus-reviewer","body":"Opus detailed review of e55536e — most critical bead in this batch because subscriptions have classic silent-failure modes.\n\nTRANSPORT WIRING: server.go:3478-3485 registers POST{} + GET{} + Websocket{} on the gqlgen handler. GET /graphql route registered at server.go:428, so WebSocket upgrade handshake actually reaches the gqlgen handler. gorilla/websocket promoted to direct dep in go.mod.\n\nRESOLVER (resolver_sub_worker.go:20):\n- ProgressSubscriber interface at L13: SubscribeProgress(workerID) (<-chan agent.ProgressEvent, func()) — WorkerManager satisfies natively (workers.go:623, exact signature, no shim)\n- Calls r.Workers.SubscribeProgress(workerID) to get source channel + unsub cleanup\n- Goroutine fans events from src to buffered out chan *WorkerEvent (cap 16), maps Phase/Message/BeadID/TS/EventID/WorkerID to GraphQL shape\n- DUAL ctx.Done() select arms: one on receive (L33), one on send (L55). Drift risk if server.go:handleGraphQLQuery diverges from test helper. Shape debt -> refactor bead.\n\nVERDICT: ACCEPT. .ddx/executions/20260504T220127-177c17c0/manifest.json:573:          "body": "Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs. ## Why this attempt declined to commit The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\" ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) \"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate). - More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes. The bead's \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work. ## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo) Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable): ### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID) Scope: - Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env). - Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works. - Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl. - Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess. - Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path. - Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection. - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green. Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC. ### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec Scope: - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct. - Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling. - Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal. Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green. Estimated: ~300–500 LOC. ### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude) Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires: - Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port. - Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead. - POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once. - Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status). - Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH. Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented. Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra. ### Dependencies - 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec) - 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape) ## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree. ## Bookkeeping A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.",

.ddx/beads.jsonl:66:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.\n\n2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**\n   - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).\n   - Start one worker for each project.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-1\u003e)` returns exactly worker 1.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-2\u003e)` returns exactly worker 2.\n   - `workers` (global) returns both.\n   - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.\n\n3. **Regression test — backward compat.**\n   - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.\n\n4. **End-to-end validation.**\n   - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. Assert exactly one new row with `state: running` and matching worker id.\n   - Same test run against a second project asserts that project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.\n\n5. **No regression to unrelated surfaces.**\n   - Global `workers` query still returns all workers.\n   - Worker detail page still loads by worker id.\n   - Workers live-progress subscription still patches rows in place.\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Note on `ddx-69789664` (Workers lifecycle controls): that bead's acceptance assumed Workers pane worked correctly. Point to this bead as the prerequisite for those tests to be meaningful.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"a21580e12b66f3b5de29b066c0dc5bfff3205f61","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:01:37.307108559Z","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.\n\nVerified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.\n\n```\n$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json\n{\n  \"id\": \"worker-20260422T205846-f23f\",\n  \"kind\": \"execute-loop\",\n  \"state\": \"running\",\n  \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",\n  ...\n}\n```\n\nSo the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:\n\n```go\nif projectID != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.\n- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.\n\nThese are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.\n\nThe global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `\"\"` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.\n\nNote: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.\n\n### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site\n\n```go\nexpectedPath := \"\"\nif projectID != \"\" {\n    proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)\n    if !ok {\n        return nil // unknown project id → no workers\n    }\n    expectedPath = proj.Path\n}\n...\nif expectedPath != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record\n\nAdd `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).\n\nRecommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.\n\n## Scope note\n\nThis is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).\n- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:01:46.806289388Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. ADR explicitly evaluates Proposals A, B, C (or articulates a fourth) and documents the chosen design with rationale.\n3. ADR includes the worker-server API contract: full request/response schemas for register, heartbeat, next-bead, event, result, disconnect.\n4. ADR includes sequence diagrams (text/mermaid) for: worker registration, bead claim, execute+report, server-restart recovery, graceful shutdown.\n5. ADR includes a compatibility analysis: what existing tests break, what behaviors migrate, what's a breaking change for operators.\n6. ADR specifies how --local mode collapses into the same code path (in-process API impl).\n7. ADR specifies sequencing relative to: ddx-29058e2a (spec unification), ddx-5cb6e6cd refactor children, ddx-dc157075 (stay-alive — likely subsumed).\n8. ADR includes implementation roadmap as an ordered list (with proposed bead titles, NOT actual filed beads — those come after ADR review).\n9. FEAT-006 or new FEAT amended to describe the worker contract (whichever is more appropriate per existing HELIX layout).\n10. Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).\n\nPROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT\nWORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to \"worker registers with itself\" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.\n\nTHE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG\n\nPROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients\n- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)\n- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)\n- Worker polls /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server\n- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event {kind, body}\n- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/result {bead-id, status, evidence}\n- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters \"disconnected\" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return\n- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead\n\nPROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous\n- Worker runs independently; no server required\n- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort\n- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator\n- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)\n- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel\n\nPROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present\n- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup\n- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue\n- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store\n- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)\n\nUNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)\n\nPOST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }\nGET  /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/disconnect → 204 (graceful)\n\nThe existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.\n\nOUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)\n\nABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):\n- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.\n- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.\n- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.\n\nCONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:\n- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.\n- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.\n- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to \"server-side preflight\" decision in API design.\n- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.\n- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.\n- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.\n\nPARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):\n- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.\n- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.\n- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.\n- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.\n- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.\n- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.\n\nSPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)\n\n- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md\n- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section,
