.ddx/executions/20260502T205525-d2423a87/prompt.md:5:Author docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md.

Source material (consume in order):
1. /tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md — full integration plan with Axon's external interface mapped + GraphQL pivot + locked decisions
2. Cross-repo tracking bead axon-82b6f7b2 — full DDx-on-axon adoption checklist (links to all required axon-side capabilities)

LOCKED DECISIONS (do not relitigate; bake into TD):
1. Deployment: separate axon-server, operator-managed. DDx does NOT spawn or supervise Axon.
2. Auth: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. Axon already supports ts-net per its serve.rs (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags). Reuses ADR-006.
3. Events storage: separate ddx_bead_events collection with event_of links (Option B). Two collections in Axon.
4. Offline posture: refuse all bead ops when Axon unreachable. No local cache, no WAL.
5. Wire transport: GraphQL only via genqlient-generated Go client. NOT gRPC. Subscriptions over WebSocket for live updates.

Cross-repo dependencies (tracked in axon-82b6f7b2):
- axon-05c1019d (P2): GraphQL pattern-query support — needed for single-query ddx bead ready/blocked. Day 1 ships with two-phase fallback.
- FEAT-013 secondary indexes (P1 Draft in axon): needed for status-field query performance at >1000 beads.
- FEAT-017 schema evolution (P1 Draft in axon): needed for clean DDx schema upgrades over time. Day 1 ships with DDx-side lazy migration on read.
- ts-net auth: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon. No coordination needed.
- GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon.
- Multi-namespace/database: ALREADY SHIPPED.

Adoption gates (per axon-82b6f7b2):
- Alpha: requires axon-05c1019d + everything already-shipped. Acceptable to ship with table-scan perf.
- Beta: requires FEAT-013 (indexes) for >1000-bead performance.
- GA: requires FEAT-013 + FEAT-017 + axon-05c1019d all landed.

Defaults the TD can adopt without rechecking with operator (revisit only if implementation surfaces a problem):
- Schema versioning: DDx-side lazy migrate on read until FEAT-017 lands; switch to axon-native then.
- ddx bead ready Day 1: two-phase GraphQL query.
- Backup/DR: operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.

Output: TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md with sections for: GraphQL schema mapping (DDx bead → Axon entity); operation mapping (DDx CLI → GraphQL queries/mutations/subscriptions); deployment + config; concurrency model (OCC via expectedVersion); migration plan; test plan; cross-repo coordination notes (links to axon-82b6f7b2 + FEAT-013/017).

Forcing\n  a commit here would land non-functional scaffolding (the failure mode\n  the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was already BLOCKED for).\n\n  Specific gaps observed in the current worktree (base ab0a056a):\n\n  1. Dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 (\"Introduce selectable bead Backend\n     interface in cli/internal/bead\") is still status=open. The current\n     cli/internal/bead/backend.go defines only the legacy interface:\n\n         Backend interface {\n             Init() error\n             ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)\n             WriteAll(beads []Bead) error\n             WithLock(fn func() error) error\n         }\n\n     TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md\n     §\"Backend interface (Go)\") explicitly says this interface is \"too\n     coarse for Axon — WriteAll(beads []Bead) cannot reasonably be\n     implemented against a remote entity store\" and prescribes a richer\n     per-entity surface (Get/Create/Update with OCC Version,\n     AppendEvent, AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import,\n     ErrVersionMismatch, etc.). Implementing the axon backend against\n     the legacy 4-method interface would (a) violate TD-030 and (b)\n     produce a backend that cannot honor claim/OCC semantics that the\n     bead description and AC #3 require (\"plus claim semantics and\n     event append\"). This bead's \u003cdescription\u003e says it \"follows the TD\n     authored in the design child\" — that TD assumes the interface\n     refactor has landed first.\n\n  2. No Axon Go SDK or generated gRPC client exists in this repo.\n     /home/erik/Projects/axon ships axon.proto at\n     crates/axon-server/proto/axon.proto (377 lines, AxonService with\n     CreateEntity/GetEntity/UpdateEntity/CommitTransaction/Traverse/\n     QueryEntities/PutSchema/CreateCollection/etc.) but no Go bindings.\n     TD-030 §\"Wire transport\" specifies generation via\n     protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc into cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/\n     with a `make gen-axon-client` target and a checked-in axon.proto\n     plus sha256. None of that scaffolding exists; protoc toolchain\n     setup, proto vendoring, and the make target are themselves a\n     non-trivial chunk of work that should be its own bead.\n\n  3. AC #5 (\"ddx bead commands work end-to-end against the axon\n     backend on a fresh repo, no migration required\") cannot be\n     verified inside this worktree. Per TD-030 §D1 the operator runs a\n     separate axon-server process; per §D4 the backend refuses all ops\n     when Axon is unreachable. There is no Axon server running in this\n     execution environment and no fixture / test-double that the AC\n     would accept (\"end-to-end ... on a fresh repo\" rules out an\n     in-memory fake).\n\n  Additional context: the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was BLOCKED in\n  post-merge review on 2026-05-02 for landing only execution evidence\n  with no implementation, then decomposed into seven children. This\n  bead is one of those children but was queued out of dependency order\n  — ddx-bbdd7564 (the interface refactor child) needs to land first.\n\nwhat_is_done:\n  - Read TD-030 (the design child's output) end-to-end.\n  - Confirmed current Backend interface in cli/internal/bead/backend.go\n    is the legacy form, not the TD-030 form.\n  - Confirmed dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 is still open.\n  - Confirmed no axon proto/client is vendored under cli/internal/bead/.\n\nwhat_is_blocking:\n  - ddx-bbdd7564 must land first to introduce the per-entity Backend\n    interface (Get/Create/Update with Version/OCC, AppendEvent,\n    AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import, error sentinels)\n    that this bead's axon implementation needs to satisfy.\n  - A separate bead should establish the gRPC client generation\n    pipeline: vendor cli/internal/bead/axon/proto/axon.proto from the\n    axon repo (with sha256 pin), add the make gen-axon-client target,\n    add tooling docs for protoc-gen-go / protoc-gen-go-grpc, and\n    commit the generated cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/ tree. This is\n    explicitly called out by TD-030 §\"Wire transport\" but is not in\n    the current bead's scope and has no existing bead covering it.\n  - AC #5 needs a clarified verification path: either (a) require a\n    running Axon test server in CI (with the deployment work that\n    implies), or (b) loosen \"end-to-end\" to mean \"round-trip against\n    a vendored Axon test harness\" and add the harness as a bead.\n\nwhat_a_followup_would_need:\n  - Re-queue this bead (ddx-c9ef6c4f) only after ddx-bbdd7564 closes\n    with the TD-030-shaped Backend interface in place.\n  - Add (and close) a new prerequisite bead for the axon gRPC client\n    generation: vendor proto, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit\n    generated stubs under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.\n  - Add a new prerequisite bead for the Axon test harness used by AC\n    #5 (either a test-only docker-compose entry, a `go test`-managed\n    server-under-test, or an explicit \"skip if AXON_ADDR unset\"\n    integration-test gate per the project's existing test-tier\n    conventions).\n  - Then this bead becomes a focused implementation: wrap the\n    generated client in cli/internal/bead/axon/, register the\n    \"axon\" backend in cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plumb the\n    DDX_BEAD_BACKEND / .ddx/config.yaml selector, and add the\n    create/claim/close/dep unit tests AC #4 names (against the\n    harness from the new prerequisite bead).",

.ddx/executions/20260415T072233-536841ac/prompt.md:5:Two minor cleanups from opus review of Stage 2.11 and 2.12:

1. bead.WatcherHub goroutines leak on server shutdown. Server.New at cli/internal/server/server.go:63 creates s.beadHub via bead.NewWatcherHub, but Server has no Shutdown/Close method that invokes s.beadHub.Close(). On process exit the goroutines terminate with the process, but on graceful Shutdown (never implemented today, but will be needed for multi-test scenarios and systemd restart with drain) the watchers leak. Add a Server.Shutdown method and invoke s.beadHub.Close() + coordinatorRegistry.StopAll() from it.

2. Subscription test helpers duplicate ~90 lines of graphql-transport-ws WebSocket setup across 3 test files: newGraphQLHandlerWithBeadBus, newGraphQLHandlerWithExecLogs, newGraphQLHandlerWithCoordMetrics. Each does the same httptest.NewServer + gqlgen config + transport registration. Extract to a single test helper newGraphQLSubscriptionTestServer(resolver *Resolver) or equivalent that takes a fully-populated resolver and returns the server + dial function. Cosmetic cleanup, makes adding Stage 3+ subscription tests cheap.

Not blocking anything. File under the migration epic.

.ddx/executions/20260429T175840-c8156ca7/manifest.json:15:    "description": "## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)\n\nOriginal plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.\n\n## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)\n\n**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.\n\n**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.\n\n**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.\n\n## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)\n\nThis bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:\n\n- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.\n- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.\n- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.\n- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for \"no viable candidate\" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.\n- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.\n- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and \"best provider\" contract.\n\nOnce that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.\n\n## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)\n\n**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**\n\n`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: \"default\"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond \"profile=default if unset.\"\n\n**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**\n\nDefault `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.\n\n**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**\n\nAfter `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.\n\n**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**\n\nDeliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:\n- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.\n- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.\n- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.\n- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.\n\nMigration note published in DDx changelog.\n\n**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**\n\n- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")`. No other DDx inputs.\n- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.\n- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Harness: \"claude\")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.\n- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\", Model: \"opus-4.7\")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.\n- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.\n- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.\n\n## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)\n\n- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).\n- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.\n\n## Sequencing\n\n1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)\n2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.\n3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.\n4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.\n5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.\n\n## Cross-references\n\n- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.\n- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.\n- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.\n- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): \"kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match.\"\n- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):\n  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)\n  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)\n  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)\n- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).\n\n## Codex review — key corrections folded in\n\n1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just \"fix ExactPin semantics.\"\n2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.\n3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.\n4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.\n5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.\n6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).\n7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.\n8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.\n9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.\n10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.",
.ddx/executions/20260429T175840-c8156ca7/prompt.md:5:## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.

.ddx/executions/20260429T152547-cea40d0b/prompt.md:5:## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.

.ddx/executions/20260429T065203-5c940767/prompt.md:5:## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.

.ddx/executions/20260428T223713-1faefc18/prompt.md:5:## Observed / Motivation

User wants to "lift work out of the TUI entirely and allow non-tmux users to work remotely." Today the only way to prompt the DDx agent stack interactively is a terminal session. The web UI is read-mostly: dispatch three actions (drain/align/checks) and review results. There is no surface that lets an operator say "go do X" against the project in natural language from a browser.

Related UX: the project home page already exists (`/nodes/.../projects/<id>/`), renders queue summary + actions, and is the natural host for a chat affordance. First the home page needs to be reachable from the sidebar (`ddx-2513f08c`) — this bead depends on that work.

## The tension

The DDx harness abstraction (`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go`, `service.Execute`) is designed for **non-interactive** use: prompt in, structured result out, optional streaming. This is the right shape for queue-draining, quorum, evaluation, and automation. It is **not** the shape of a TUI conversation with tab completion, mid-run steering, and back-channel signals.

An operator-facing chat can land on either side of that line. The bead records all four options so the design phase can pick one deliberately.

## Options (design phase decides, recommended path noted)

### Option A — Chat over the harness abstraction (RECOMMENDED starting point)

- Chat UI posts a prompt → server dispatches a session via `service.Execute` (or a wrapper) → streams tool calls + response chunks back over a GraphQL subscription.
- Message history is persisted as a thread; each user turn = one new session. Responses reuse the existing `recentEvents` stream shape already used on the workers detail page.
- Works with every harness today because the harness abstraction is what it is. claude, codex, agent (embedded) all participate.
- **Trade-off:** no mid-run steering, no tab completion, no live back-and-forth inside one agent invocation.
- Matches the user's stated target audience (non-TUI users who want remote browser access). Those users aren't trying to steer a TUI — they're trying to leave it behind.

### Option B — PTY gateway per harness

- Server owns a PTY pool. Each chat thread attaches to one harness process (claude/codex/etc.) over a WebSocket; browser renders xterm.js.
- Full interactivity, including TUI steering.
- **Trade-off:** PTY lifecycle, auth, resource limits, tab-close-loses-session semantics, multi-user access control — all real. The harness abstraction is bypassed for this mode, which creates a second execution path to maintain.

### Option C — Hybrid (chat-first + pop-out terminal)

- Option A is the primary surface. When a chat reaches a point that needs interactive steering, a "Open terminal" escape hatch attaches a PTY to the current harness session.
- **Trade-off:** more surface area, but honest about both use cases. Most sessions stay on the cheap path.

### Option D — Web-tmux / ttyd wrapper

- Embed the existing terminal tooling directly: ttyd or xterm.js over a tmux socket owned by the server.
- **Trade-off:** fastest to ship, zero harness work, but the UX is a terminal in a browser — not "chat." Requires tmux. Doesn't serve the stated "non-tmux users" audience.

### Recommendation

Start with **A**. If the design note documents real interactive-steering needs we can't paper over, the right follow-up is **C** (A + PTY escape hatch), not B (PTY-only).

## Scope of this bead

- Design note in `docs/helix/02-design/` locking the option, the message schema, persistence model, and auth posture. Implementation PR links to it.
- First implementation pass: Option A.
- Home page gains a chat pane as its primary interaction surface, alongside the existing queue summary + actions (which stay — they're quick-dispatch affordances, not replaced by chat).

## Subsidiary questions the design note must answer

1. **Message persistence.** Are chat threads per-project or per-user? Stored where (`.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/` or elsewhere)? Is history shared across operators on the same project, or isolated per operator identity?
2. **Harness selection.** Does the chat pick a harness per-thread (picker in the UI) or per-message (re-route each turn)? Default to profile-based selection?
3. **Cost accountability.** Every chat message spawns a session. Sessions are already tracked in the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). The chat thread should surface cumulative cost to the operator so they don't blow budget unknowingly.
4. **Tool execution scope.** Chat prompts will trigger agent tool calls that read/write the project tree. Is the chat session bounded to the same permissions as `ddx agent run`? Does it run in the project worktree, a worktree per thread, a scratch dir? Default safe answer: same worktree as `ddx agent run` (user trusts ddx running anyway), but design note records it.
5. **Concurrency.** What happens if two operators have the chat open on the same project and both send messages? Default: independent threads, each with its own session — no shared state.
6. **Auth.** Today the server assumes single-operator localhost. Remote use implies some identity concept. Design note picks a posture: delegate to reverse proxy, ship a simple token, or punt and require tsnet + single-operator for v1.

## Out of scope

- Full PTY implementation (Option B). Follow-up bead if A+hybrid doesn't suffice.
- Cross-project chat.
- Voice input.
.ddx/executions/20260428T223713-1faefc18/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As an operator using a browser — on a machine without a terminal, or a phone, or a laptop away from my dev box — I can open my project's home page, type a prompt into a chat pane, and see the DDx agent work on it in real time. Tool calls stream as they happen, costs accrue visibly, and the conversation persists so I can come back to it.

**Acceptance criteria (written for Option A; rewrite if design note chooses B/C/D):**

1. **Design note landed.** One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` records the chosen option, the answers to the six subsidiary questions, and the message/thread schema. Implementation PR links to it.

2. **Thread model.** Chat threads persist under `.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}`. One thread per project per operator by default; operators can create named threads. Messages.jsonl is append-only, one JSON object per message (user or assistant), with `sessionId` linking each assistant turn back to an entry in the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`).

3. **GraphQL surface.** New resolvers:
   - Query: `chatThreads(projectId)`, `chatThread(id)`, `chatMessages(threadId, since?)`.
   - Mutation: `chatThreadCreate(projectId, title?)`, `chatSend(threadId, message, harness?, profile?)` → kicks off a session via `service.Execute`.
   - Subscription: `chatStream(threadId)` → emits assistant message chunks + tool call events (same event vocabulary as `workers.recentEvents`).

4. **Home page UI.** Project home page gains a chat pane below the actions section. Pane shows thread list, active thread messages, input box, harness/profile picker. Tool calls render inline within assistant messages. Streaming updates as chunks arrive.

5. **Session linkage.** Each assistant turn creates one session in the sessions index. Clicking an assistant message shows the session id and a link to the session detail (`/sessions/<id>`). Chat thread header shows cumulative session count + total cost for the thread.

6. **Concurrency.** Two browsers open on the same thread see each other's messages within 2s (subscription-driven). Two operators on different threads in the same project do not interfere.

7. **Interrupt.** The in-flight assistant turn can be cancelled via a Stop button; server sends the harness the equivalent of `ddx agent workers stop <id>` for the in-flight session. The thread records a cancelled turn, does not try to auto-resume.

8. **Cost guardrail (soft).** If the cumulative thread cost passes a configurable ceiling (`.ddx/config.yaml: chat.cost_ceiling_usd`, default unset), the UI warns but does not block. Hard block is a follow-up.

9. **Auth posture.**
   - Per design note. If the chosen posture is "single-operator localhost / tsnet only for v1," the chat routes are served unauthenticated behind tsnet and the design note records the gap to close before broader deployment.
   - A small middleware gate is in place even for v1 so adding auth later is schema-local, not route-local.

10. **Playwright e2e.**
    - Seeds a project with zero chat threads.
    - Navigates to project home, asserts the chat pane renders with "Start a new thread" affordance.
    - Starts a thread, sends a short prompt, simulates a streamed session response with one tool call, asserts chunks appear in order and the session id link is present.
    - Opens a second browser context on the same thread, asserts both see new messages within 2s.
    - Cancels an in-flight turn, asserts the cancelled state is persisted.

11. **Depends on.**
    - `ddx-2513f08c` (home page reachable from sidebar) — hard dependency.
    - `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) — hard dependency for the session linkage + cost rollup.

12. **Explicitly deferred.** PTY integration (Option B / pop-out terminal from Option C) is its own bead, filed only if the Option A rollout surfaces real interactive-steering needs.
.ddx/executions/20260428T032051-2dd22d56/prompt.md:5:## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.
.ddx/executions/20260428T223713-1faefc18/manifest.json:13:    "description": "## Observed / Motivation\n\nUser wants to \"lift work out of the TUI entirely and allow non-tmux users to work remotely.\" Today the only way to prompt the DDx agent stack interactively is a terminal session. The web UI is read-mostly: dispatch three actions (drain/align/checks) and review results. There is no surface that lets an operator say \"go do X\" against the project in natural language from a browser.\n\nRelated UX: the project home page already exists (`/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/`), renders queue summary + actions, and is the natural host for a chat affordance. First the home page needs to be reachable from the sidebar (`ddx-2513f08c`) — this bead depends on that work.\n\n## The tension\n\nThe DDx harness abstraction (`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go`, `service.Execute`) is designed for **non-interactive** use: prompt in, structured result out, optional streaming. This is the right shape for queue-draining, quorum, evaluation, and automation. It is **not** the shape of a TUI conversation with tab completion, mid-run steering, and back-channel signals.\n\nAn operator-facing chat can land on either side of that line. The bead records all four options so the design phase can pick one deliberately.\n\n## Options (design phase decides, recommended path noted)\n\n### Option A — Chat over the harness abstraction (RECOMMENDED starting point)\n\n- Chat UI posts a prompt → server dispatches a session via `service.Execute` (or a wrapper) → streams tool calls + response chunks back over a GraphQL subscription.\n- Message history is persisted as a thread; each user turn = one new session. Responses reuse the existing `recentEvents` stream shape already used on the workers detail page.\n- Works with every harness today because the harness abstraction is what it is. claude, codex, agent (embedded) all participate.\n- **Trade-off:** no mid-run steering, no tab completion, no live back-and-forth inside one agent invocation.\n- Matches the user's stated target audience (non-TUI users who want remote browser access). Those users aren't trying to steer a TUI — they're trying to leave it behind.\n\n### Option B — PTY gateway per harness\n\n- Server owns a PTY pool. Each chat thread attaches to one harness process (claude/codex/etc.) over a WebSocket; browser renders xterm.js.\n- Full interactivity, including TUI steering.\n- **Trade-off:** PTY lifecycle, auth, resource limits, tab-close-loses-session semantics, multi-user access control — all real. The harness abstraction is bypassed for this mode, which creates a second execution path to maintain.\n\n### Option C — Hybrid (chat-first + pop-out terminal)\n\n- Option A is the primary surface. When a chat reaches a point that needs interactive steering, a \"Open terminal\" escape hatch attaches a PTY to the current harness session.\n- **Trade-off:** more surface area, but honest about both use cases. Most sessions stay on the cheap path.\n\n### Option D — Web-tmux / ttyd wrapper\n\n- Embed the existing terminal tooling directly: ttyd or xterm.js over a tmux socket owned by the server.\n- **Trade-off:** fastest to ship, zero harness work, but the UX is a terminal in a browser — not \"chat.\" Requires tmux. Doesn't serve the stated \"non-tmux users\" audience.\n\n### Recommendation\n\nStart with **A**. If the design note documents real interactive-steering needs we can't paper over, the right follow-up is **C** (A + PTY escape hatch), not B (PTY-only).\n\n## Scope of this bead\n\n- Design note in `docs/helix/02-design/` locking the option, the message schema, persistence model, and auth posture. Implementation PR links to it.\n- First implementation pass: Option A.\n- Home page gains a chat pane as its primary interaction surface, alongside the existing queue summary + actions (which stay — they're quick-dispatch affordances, not replaced by chat).\n\n## Subsidiary questions the design note must answer\n\n1. **Message persistence.** Are chat threads per-project or per-user? Stored where (`.ddx/chat/\u003cthread-id\u003e/` or elsewhere)? Is history shared across operators on the same project, or isolated per operator identity?\n2. **Harness selection.** Does the chat pick a harness per-thread (picker in the UI) or per-message (re-route each turn)? Default to profile-based selection?\n3. **Cost accountability.** Every chat message spawns a session. Sessions are already tracked in the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). The chat thread should surface cumulative cost to the operator so they don't blow budget unknowingly.\n4. **Tool execution scope.** Chat prompts will trigger agent tool calls that read/write the project tree. Is the chat session bounded to the same permissions as `ddx agent run`? Does it run in the project worktree, a worktree per thread, a scratch dir? Default safe answer: same worktree as `ddx agent run` (user trusts ddx running anyway), but design note records it.\n5. **Concurrency.** What happens if two operators have the chat open on the same project and both send messages? Default: independent threads, each with its own session — no shared state.\n6. **Auth.** Today the server assumes single-operator localhost. Remote use implies some identity concept. Design note picks a posture: delegate to reverse proxy, ship a simple token, or punt and require tsnet + single-operator for v1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Full PTY implementation (Option B). Follow-up bead if A+hybrid doesn't suffice.\n- Cross-project chat.\n- Voice input.",

.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/prompt.md:5:## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.

.ddx/executions/20260415T083712-f69fc612/prompt.md:5:Opus Stage 3 batch review (2026-04-15): Houdini 1.5.10 has unresolved peer-dep incompatibility with vite 8 + kit 2.57. STACK.md recommended Option A (downgrade vite) or Option B (drop Houdini for graphql-request). Stage 3.4 shipped with neither — works for now because the single NodeInfo query is trivial.

Stage 4.12 Workers page requires graphql-ws subscriptions (workerProgress). Houdini 1.5.10 subscription support on Vite 8 is the most likely place the peer-dep workaround falls apart.

Decision required BEFORE Stage 4.12: either (a) migrate to houdini@next (removes the 'upgrade to houdini@next' noise and fixes Vite 8 support), or (b) drop Houdini entirely for graphql-request + graphql-ws (lower abstraction, no codegen).
.ddx/executions/20260415T083712-f69fc612/prompt.md:8:(1) Decision recorded in STACK.md. (4) Stage 4.12 workerProgress subscription round-trips in a smoke test.

No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.
